evals.prompt: Start an agent discussion on the topic of 'Python asyncio vs threading for building web scrapers' using the file /tmp/discussion-async.md. Your agent name is claude. Write your intro and then stop (set [TURN: NEXT] and do not continue polling).
evals.expected_output: A discussion file at /tmp/discussion-async.md with a well-researched Intro citing specific technical facts (e.g. GIL behavior, asyncio event loop overhead, real benchmark numbers, specific library names like aiohttp/httpx/requests) gathered via web search or documentation lookup before writing.
expectations: The agent performs at least one research action (web search, documentation lookup, or similar) before writing the intro
The intro mentions at least one specific technical detail that goes beyond common knowledge (e.g. GIL behavior details, named libraries, benchmark figures, specific Python version behaviors)
The intro is at least 100 words long
The intro contains no first-person advocacy ('I think', 'I believe', 'I prefer')
The discussion file exists at /tmp/discussion-async.md with correct format including [TURN: NEXT]

evals.prompt: Start an agent discussion on the topic of 'monorepo vs polyrepo for a 5-person Node.js team' using the file /tmp/discussion-monorepo.md. Your agent name is claude. Explore the current repository at /home/cyrus/workspace/agent-discussion to understand the project structure before writing your intro. Write your intro and then stop (set [TURN: NEXT] and do not continue polling).
evals.expected_output: A discussion file at /tmp/discussion-monorepo.md with an Intro that is grounded in research — either codebase exploration (what the actual repo structure reveals) or external information about tooling (nx, turborepo, changesets, etc.), not just generic platitudes.
expectations: The agent explores the repository (uses Glob, Grep, Read, or Bash ls) before writing the intro

The intro references at least one concrete detail from research — either a named tool (nx, turborepo, lerna, changesets, pnpm workspaces), a specific tradeoff with evidence, or an observation about the actual repo
The intro is at least 100 words long
The intro contains no first-person advocacy
The discussion file exists at /tmp/discussion-monorepo.md with correct format including [TURN: NEXT]
